title: grumilea weberi
intro: Grumilea weberi is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the hairy mint bush due to its hairy leaves and mint-like fragrance. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various herbal preparations for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Grumilea weberi include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Grumilea weberi is often prepared as a tea or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties, although its efficacy and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied.
